briefing.headline: Rig up a Drop Shot — aggressive fall approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Drop Shot is a strong choice for fall with muddy water and pre frontal conditions.

Johnston: On deep Lake Ontario smallmouth at 33 feet, use a drop shot with a 3/8-ounce weight in all conditions. On calm days, cast it out and let it sit — don't move it. On rough, windy days, cast past the fish and drag it through them.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, creek channel, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Johnston: Use a heavier drop shot weight up-current from structure to keep the bait down in current. In slacker water behind the structure, use a lighter weight for the same presentation, which is imp
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, creek channel, rock. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Johnston: Fall smallmouth fishing is all about location and timing. Cover water fast with reaction baits to find active groups, then slow down with finesse presentations to catch the rest. If fish stop biting a tube, switch to Ned rig or drop shot.
Palaniuk: 'For the Santee Cooper drop shot, I used a green/pumpkin/blue X-Zone Deception Worm 12 inches above the weight on an Alpha Angler DSR 6-foot-10 medium rod with a Daiwa Exist 3000 reel.'
Robertson: Won the Cherokee Lake Open with a drop shot and topwater combination during post-tropical-storm, post-frontal fall conditions.
Wheeler: In fall, fish school up in ditches, on little flats in 2-4 feet, and especially around grass; on grassless lakes, key on shallow ditches with stumps that let bass ambush shad.

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal fall bass are following shad into the backs of creeks. Fish can be anywhere from 3-18ft depending on bait position. Target creek arms, secondary points, and anywhere baitfish are concentrating.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight
